message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: My task: add with_counts param to groupby filter in filters.py. This is the only file I need to touch.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: I am implementing case_sensitive param for groupby() filter in src/jinja2/filters.py. This is likely the same file you need to touch - let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections or sequence our edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both must edit the same do_groupby/do_async_groupby functions in filters.py, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ownership instead - I'll add case_sensitive param first (small, isolated change to sort key + grouping), send DONE, then you add with_counts on top of my version. I'll start now and ping you when done. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed - we both need groupby() in src/jinja2/filters.py, unavoidable overlap. To keep merges clean: use additive keyword-only-by-convention params appended in a fixed order: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False, with_counts=False). You add case_sensitive, I add with_counts, appended after yours. I won't touch case_sensitive logic; please don't touch with_counts logic. I'll implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: OK, sounds good. I'll wait for your DONE before finalizing, and will implement with_counts as an additive param appended after case_sensitive. Go ahead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Agreed on param order and logic ownership. IMPORTANT: since we're editing the exact same file/functions concurrently, simultaneous edits will clobber each other via read-modify-write races. Please hold off editing filters.py until I send DONE - I'm making a small isolated change and will ping you within a couple minutes so you can pull the latest file before adding with_counts.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished adding case_sensitive param to sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (async) in src/jinja2/filters.py. Both functions now have signature (environment, value, attribute, default=None, case_sensitive=False). All 837 existing tests pass. You can now pull the latest file and append with_counts=False after case_sensitive in both function signatures/bodies. Patch written to patch.txt.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Added with_counts=False param to sync_do_groupby and do_groupby (async) in src/jinja2/filters.py, appended after default (I don't have case_sensitive in my copy since we work in separate sandboxes, but the param is additive so should compose fine). When with_counts=True, yields plain (grouper, items, count) tuples instead of _GroupTuple; default behavior unchanged. All 163 filter tests pass. Patch written to my patch.txt.
